The video demonstrates three members of the same -erally family in stacked text boxes; "Coviderally" is the flagship/title word, with "Legiterally" (legit + literally) and "Miderally" (mid + literally) shown beneath it as related forms — these are recorded as additional parts of speech and as synonyms. All three usage examples are Sendy's actual caption sentences (SRT), lightly cleaned: the milk/McDonald's drive-through line, the flat-earth/triangle line, and the backhanded-compliment line. ENRICHMENT (mine, not literally said by Sendy): sub-sense 1b framing Coviderally as a marker of pandemic-era recollection specifically; the generalized "-erally" productivity note in the etymology and usage note; the adverbial part-of-speech labels and IPA. Captions render "coviderally," "Legiterally," and "miderally" in lowercase mid-sentence, consistent with ordinary adverb capitalization; frame title-cases them as headwords.

etymology: A blend (portmanteau) of COW + OWL, fusing the two animals by overlapping their shared medial diphthong /aʊ/. The premise rests on a chain of onomatopoeia: an owl is said to make the sound "hoo" and a cow the sound "moo"; the hybrid creature therefore yields a hybrid call. Homophonous with the unrelated English noun cowl ("a hooded garment; a chimney covering"), though Sendy's coinage is wholly independent of that sense.

senses.def: A hypothetical hybrid animal compounded of a cow and an owl, invoked chiefly as the subject of the question of what sound it would make.

examples.text: An owl makes "hoo" and a cow makes "moo," so naturally one wonders what sound a cowl makes.

subsenses.def: The creature itself, conceived as bearing the body of one parent and the cry of the other.

example.text: The cowl, being half bovine and half owl, grazes by day and hoots by night.

senses.def: The vocalization produced by such a creature: a blended call combining the lowing "moo" of the cow with the hooting "hoo" of the owl, rendered approximately as "hoom" or "moo-hoo."

examples.text: He drew breath, screwed up his face, and delivered the sound a cowl makes.

notes: On-screen headword is authoritatively spelled "Cowl" (black title box, frames f01-f06; also confirmed in the description, "The Sound a Cowl makes"). The video uses Sendy's animated word-by-word caption style rather than his usual (Root + Root) blue etymology boxes; the on-screen build-up text reads "OWL MAKES HOO" (f01) and "COW MAKES MOO" (f03), making the Cow + Owl derivation explicit and unambiguous. The SRT confirms this exactly ("The sound an owl makes Hoo" / "The sound a cow makes Moo" / "The sound a cowl makes"). The punchline sound itself is NOT spelled on screen or in the captions; the final frames (f05-f06) show Sendy delivering it as a deadpan vocal performance. ENRICHMENT (mine, not literally stated by Sendy): the spelled approximations "hoom"/"moo-hoo" for sense 2 are my reconstruction of the blended call from his Hoo+Moo logic; the noun sense 1a (the creature as a physical hybrid), the synonyms, the "homophonous with the garment/chimney noun" note, and the usage label are house-style additions consistent with his premise. The headword IPA is /kaʊl/ only; the previously listed second IPA "/haʊm/" was removed because it is the inferred creature-call, not a pronunciation of the word "cowl," and that reconstruction now lives solely in the sense-2 prose. Confidence is medium because the exact intended sound is left to performance and is inferred.

etymology: Blend (wombo) of cunning, adjective denoting cleverness or sly ingenuity, and linguist, noun denoting one skilled in or devoted to the study and use of language. The first element is run into the second by way of the shared medial -in-/-lin-, yielding a single closed compound. Coined by Professor Sendy.

senses.def: A person of conspicuous verbal dexterity; one exceptionally skilled at arranging and combining words to artful effect.

examples.text: That cunninlinguist is gonna deliver the quiche peanemis with no analvisions.
That cunninlinguist is about to get rizzioed in the huzzitorium.
That cunninlinguist will not make you deadassionately have a nonchalashout all over the brarpet; he's gonna have it out back, brorch.

subsenses.def: Specifically, one whose command of language is so deft that it appears effortless or sly; a slick or crafty turner of phrase.

example.text: Only a true cunninlinguist could string those words together like that.

notes: On-screen black text box gives the authoritative spelling "Cunninlinguist"; blue etymology boxes read "Cunning + Linguist." Auto-captions render it variably ("cunninlinguist"); trusted the frame. Sendy's stated meaning per the captions is "somebody who's really good at putting words together," which the definition paraphrases into house style. His three usage examples are themselves stuffed with other (undefined) wombos (quiche peanemis, analvisions, rizzioed, huzzitorium, deadassionately, nonchalashout, brarpet, brorch); these are reproduced as he said them and lightly cleaned into sentences. Enrichment beyond Sendy's literal statement: the "informal" usage label, the plural form, subsense 1a's gloss on the sly/effortless shade (drawing on the "cunning" root), the IPA, and the synonym list, all consistent with his meaning. The word is a well-known real-world double entendre, but the entry reflects only Sendy's stated "good with words" sense.

etymology: A blend (wombo) of don't and haven't, combining the senses of disinclination ("do not want to") and incompletion ("have not yet done"). The apostrophe is retained from both source contractions, yielding the doubly-apostrophized form D'aven't. First demonstrated by Professor Sendy in 2026.

senses.def: A combined contraction expressing simultaneously that one does not wish to perform an action and that one has not in fact performed it; the merger of 'don't' and 'haven't' into a single word.

examples.text: I d'aven't taken a shower since last Tuesday.

subsenses.def: Used to convey present disinclination toward an action (the 'don't' element): do not want to.

example.text: I d'aven't want you to take one.

subsenses.def: Used to convey that an action remains uncompleted (the 'haven't' element): have not yet done.

example.text: You d'aven't held your own.

notes: On-screen black box reads "D'aven't" with blue etymology boxes "Don't + Haven't"; the apostrophe spelling is authoritative over the auto-captions. Definition and both example sentences are drawn directly from Sendy's SRT usage ("I d'aven't taken a shower since last Tuesday," "I d'aven't want you to take one," "you d'aven't held your own"), lightly cleaned into full sentences. The two sub-senses (a: do-not-want, b: have-not-done) are house-style enrichment that formalizes the dual meaning he explicitly states ("you don't wanna do something and you also haven't done that thing"). IPA is plausible/dry house-style, not given on screen. The post also strings together several other unrelated wombos in the demo captions (peanematic, sixorseveno'clockalypse, nerpe, rizzappointed, bronut, shid) which are separate entries, not part of this definition. Video is a Speechify partnership/merch promo wrapper but the core content is a genuine wombo definition.

etymology: A nonce mega-compound assembled by Professor Sendy from a chain of his own previously coined roots and stems, fused without spacing in the manner of an agglutinative dedication. Discernible constituents include dead- and -alive- (the "dead or alive" doublet), -livin-, -lockin-, -lowing-, baun- and -ergetic (after energetic), yeet, -kinetic (Greek kīnētikós "of motion"), sender/-sender- (the Sendy stem), gibon- (after gibbon, recurring in Sendy's bestiary), -ocular (Latin oculus "eye"), -learnable, under-, -eppy-, yeak-, and -ohitic (a pseudo-scientific terminal suffix on the model of -otic/-itic). The whole functions as a single ceremonial coinage rather than a binary (Root + Root) wombo.

senses.def: A bespoke honorific mega-compound coined by Professor Sendy in tribute to a named individual, assembled by fusing a long chain of his own established roots into a single unbroken word; a birthday or milestone dedication rendered as one wombo.

subsenses.def: Specifically, the laudatory title bestowed upon Liv on her birthday, presented as a personalized salute to a member of the inner community (the Senduminati).

example.text: Happy birthday, Liv — Deadalivinlockinlowingbaunergeticyeetkineticsendergibonoculearnableundereppyeakohitic.

subsenses.def: By extension, any maximal portmanteau built entirely from one's own prior coinages, offered as a token of esteem rather than as a defined term.

example.text: He stacks every root he has ever invented into one breath and calls it a Deadalivinlockinlowingbaunergeticyeetkineticsendergibonoculearnableundereppyeakohitic.

senses.def: A piece of content produced exclusively for devoted followers; an in-group offering made for the Senduminati.

examples.text: I make videos like this for members of the Senduminati, since you guys are the real ones.

notes: No SRT/caption file exists for this video, so there is no spoken definition; the sense is reconstructed from the authoritative on-screen text box, the post description ("Happy birthday Liv: Deadalivinlockin..."), and the on-screen line "I make videos like this for members of the Senduminati." Headword spelling is taken verbatim from frame f02 (confirmed identical in f04). This is NOT a standard two-root wombo with (Root + Root) blue boxes; it is a single bespoke mega-compound, so the etymology is segmented by best-effort morphemic analysis and the constituent glosses are house-style enrichment flagged here as partly conjectural. IPA is a plausible house rendering of an unspoken word and should be treated as approximate. Confidence set to medium: the word, its dedicatory function, and its year are solid, but the precise gloss is inferred rather than transcribed. The description also promotes a related merch item ("Lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura shirt").

etymology: A compound stack-word coined by Professor Sendy, formed by fusing a chain of contemporary slang roots into a single "-sendergy" species. Built from deadass (in an utterly committed, no-pretense manner) + mental (the locked, focused mind) + lockin (locked in; total concentration) + (f)lowing (rapping or speaking with fluid rhythm) + the joining particle bau + (e)nergetic (charged with vigour) + yeet (to launch or throw forcefully) + kinetic (of or relating to motion) + sendergy, Sendy's signature suffix (a blend of the coiner's name Sendy + energy), denoting a specific, named variety of aura or radiant force. The whole therefore reads, root by root, as "the deadass mentally-locked-in flowing energetic yeet-kinetic Sendy-energy."

senses.def: The surge of forceful, motion-charged energy a person gives off immediately after fully committing to a single dramatic gesture; specifically, the aura produced when one hurls a dismissive phrase at a rival mid-performance while remaining completely focused and unbothered.

subsenses.def: The particular form of this energy generated by interrupting someone who is rapping or otherwise flowing, by yeeting the phrase "shut yo b-word ass up" at him.

example.text: You catch this bro flowing, you throw "shut yo b-word ass up" right at him, and that wave that hits you after is pure deadamentalockinlowingbaunergeticyeetkineticsendergy.

subsenses.def: By extension, the calm-yet-explosive state of being deadass committed and mentally locked in at the very same moment, so that the outburst reads as composed rather than frantic.

example.text: He looked locked in mentally in a deadass way even while going all in on the motion, which is exactly the deadamentalockinlowingbaunergeticyeetkineticsendergy.

senses.def: The energy one receives or absorbs as a result of going all in on the motion; the payoff felt by the doer after the gesture lands.

examples.text: This is the energy you get after you go all in on this motion.

notes: Headword spelling is taken verbatim from the on-screen black text box, which is authoritative; it appears identically across all six frames (wrapped to two lines: "Deadamentalockinlowingbau / nergeticyeetkineticsendergy"). The subtitle box reads only "Definition." No separate blue (Root + Root) etymology boxes appear in this video — it is one of Sendy's "-sendergy" mega-stacks, so the etymology was reconstructed by segmenting the headword into its component slang roots; that segmentation (deadass / mental / lockin / flowing / bau / nergetic / yeet / kinetic / sendergy) is my original analysis and is the chief source of uncertainty (notably the joining particle "bau", which I read as a filler/linking syllable). etymology: Blend (portmanteau) of the slang intensifier deadass ("in complete earnest, without joking"; itself dead + ass as an intensifier) and the adverb passionately. The fused medial -ass- + pass- yields the surface form -assionately-. Coined by Professor Sendy, who demonstrates the term as the seed of a recursively expandable compound, accreting the rhyming suffixes -spectacular, -particular, -lenticular, and finally -lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura to produce escalating maximal forms (Deadassionatetacular, Deadassionatetacularticular, Deadassionatetacularticulenticular, and the full Deadassionatetacularticulenticulowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura).

senses.def: with complete and unironic earnestness; in a manner combining total sincerity with ardent feeling. Used to assert that the action it modifies is undertaken both seriously and with genuine passion.

examples.text: He told her, deadassionately, that he had never meant anything more in his life.

subsenses.def: as a sincerity hedge: signalling that the speaker is not joking even while expressing strong emotion.

example.text: I am, deadassionately, the proudest I have ever been.

senses.def: in its expandable forms, used to escalate the intensity by degrees, each appended element ratcheting the earnest passion upward toward an absolute, maximal commitment.

examples.text: She did not merely agree; she agreed Deadassionatetacularticulenticulowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura.

senses.def: characterized by earnest, no-nonsense passion; sincerely and intensely heartfelt.

examples.text: It was a deadassionate confession, with no trace of irony in it.

Sense 2 (the recursive intensifying construction) and the adjective part of speech are house-style enrichment consistent with the demonstrated word-building pattern.

senses.def: Genuinely and emphatically excellent; of the highest quality in a way one means without exaggeration. A fusion of being at one's peak with the earnest insistence of deadass.

subsenses.def: (of a day, event, or experience) so good as to be sincerely worth celebrating; truly top-tier.

The constituent links, in sequence, are DEFENESTRATE → (defenestra)tion + (ban)SHEE → NOMINAL → SEMINAL → (semina)LUNAR → KIRKOLA → (kirko)la + (a)BURGER → BENTAL → INSTRUMENTAL → MINIMAL → (minima)LAURA / AURA, each junction elided into the following stem. The newest installment grafted onto the front of the running word is the defenestrate–banshee segment ("defenestrachee"). Built on the model of Sendy's other ever-lengthening composite headwords.

etymology: A blend (wombo) of DELULU (itself a reduplicative clipping of DELUSIONAL, denoting fanciful self-belief) and SIGMA (internet slang for the self-styled lone-wolf male who ranks himself outside the conventional alpha-beta hierarchy). Coined by Professor Sendy, 2026. The medial -g- is carried over from the second element, fusing the unfounded conviction of "delulu" with the aloof self-regard of "sigma." Compare the same video's companion coinages 4chenuinely (4chan + genuinely), blarping (bruh + larping), and apocalaura (apocalyptic + aura).

senses.def: The state or quality of being delusionally self-assured; baseless confidence in one's own lone-wolf superiority, in which the swagger of the self-proclaimed "sigma" rests on no factual footing.

examples.text: He thinks he's so confident, but lowkenuinely his delugma is straight up Peanema.

subsenses.def: An individual instance or display of such ungrounded self-belief.

example.text: That was pure delugma from a man who has never once been right.

senses.def: Of a person: deludedly convinced of one's own sigma-tier excellence; confidently detached from reality. (House-style extension consistent with Sendy's usage.)

examples.text: Bro is fully delugma about his chances.

etymology: Blend of delulu (clipped, reduplicated internet form of delusional) + Lululemon (proprietary name of the athletic-apparel brand) + liminal (Latin līmen, līmin- "threshold"; denoting an in-between, transitional space). The overlapping syllables of Lululemon and liminal are telescoped so that a single -minal sequence serves both roots, yielding Delulu·lemon·iminal. The liminal element is reinforced by the term's signature imagery of loitering on transitional, threshold-like architecture such as decorative balconies.

senses.def: A self-deluding state entered by purchasing Lululemon apparel in the mistaken belief that it will look flattering, when in fact it does not; the resulting condition of misplaced sartorial confidence.

examples.text: He went out and bought Lululemon delusionally thinking it was going to look good on him, but it didn't look good at all — pure Delululemoniminal.

subsenses.def: By extension, the social fallout of this state: the expectation of making friends through the wardrobe, followed instead by being repeatedly shunned and rendered unable to socialize.

example.text: You thought the outfit would help you make all these friends, but instead you just get shunned all the time.

subsenses.def: The terminal phase of the condition, in which the sufferer, frustrated and friendless, retreats to loitering in liminal, threshold-like spaces — characteristically second-story decorative balconies — for the entire day.

example.text: So you've resorted to hanging out on second-story decorative balconies all day, thanks to your Delululemoniminal wardrobe.

senses.def: Of a person, garment, or wardrobe: characterized by or marked by this state; deludedly outfitted in Lululemon and consequently isolated.

examples.text: Thanks to your Delululemoniminal wardrobe, you're up on the balcony again.

senses.def: To enter or dwell in this state; to buy and wear Lululemon under a flattering delusion and end up shunned and loitering in liminal spaces.

examples.text: He's been Delululemoniminaling on that balcony since the new leggings arrived.

etymology: A telescoping blend of "delusional" and the athletic-apparel brand "Lululemon," in which "delusional" is folded over the brand name from both ends: the opening syllables yield "delulu" (a clipping already used in internet slang for "delusional"), the brand "Lululemon" sits in the middle, and the word closes on the "-usional" of "delusional" again. The construction is recursive by design, the headword purporting to contain "delusional" twice; per its coiner the word is therefore itself delusional, since it believes it can sustain a double helping of "delusional" that it cannot in fact hold.

senses.def: of a person: delusional while conspicuously dressed in Lululemon or comparable premium athleisure; given to unfounded optimism or self-regard sustained, as it were, by one's activewear.

subsenses.def: by extension, of an idea, plan, or thing: so overconfident as to attempt the impossible; convinced of its own capacity beyond reason.

example.text: The word itself is delusional: it thinks it can handle having 'delusional' in it twice, but it can't.

senses.def: a person who is delusional and clad in Lululemon; one whose detachment from reality is matched by the price of their leggings.

examples.text: He's a total delululemonusional, manifesting a six-figure exit in two-hundred-dollar joggers.

etymology: A maximal stacked wombo assembled in stages on screen, each new root telescoped into the tail of the last: DONALD TRUMP + dump → (Trump-)dump; + inflation → DUMPFLATION; + exempt → DUMPFLATEXEMPT; + revolution → DUMPFLATEXEMPTROLUTION, with the final "-rolution" formed by eliding the shared syllable where "exempt" meets "revolution." The compound thus layers, in order, the proper name Donald Trump, "dump," "inflation," "exempt," and "revolution."

senses.def: A sweeping upheaval in which Donald Trump is held to dump the burden of rising prices onto others while declaring himself and his own exempt from it; an inflation-driven revolt framed as both the cause and the consequence of presidential price-shedding.

etymology: A four-root wombo blending elite + niche + ball + knowledge. The first element, "Eliche," fuses "elite" (superior, exclusive) with "niche" (a narrow specialty); the second, "Balledge," fuses the slang intensifier "ball" with "knowledge." The compound thus names knowledge that is simultaneously high-status, narrowly specialized, and held by few. Coined by Professor Sendy (2026).

senses.def: Specialized, exclusive knowledge; mastery of an obscure or high-status subject that few others possess.

The two are fused at the shared syllable, Emin(em) + (r)ap + god, yielding the conflated persona Eminapgod. Coined by Professor Sendy.

etymology: Blend (wombo) of ENIGMA (a puzzle; something obscure or inexplicable) + CINEMA (the medium of film). Coined by Professor Sendy. The on-screen black-box headword reads "Enima," with the blue root boxes giving "Enigma + Cinema." Pronounced and spelled so that the final element overlaps the -nema of cinema with the -nigma of enigma, yielding a single trisyllable. Compare the extended derivative ENIMANEM, in which the rapper EMINEM is folded into the same blend.

senses.def: A piece of cinema or filmed media that is profoundly confusing or hard to follow; a movie, scene, or screen moment whose meaning is obscure to the viewer.

subsenses.def: By extension, the bewildered state produced by watching such material; cinematic mystification.

etymology: Blend (wombo) of EXQUISITE (extremely beautiful, refined, or delicately fashioned) and QUICHE (a savory open-faced custard tart of egg and cream, typically baked in pastry). Coined by Professor Sendy; the medial -quis- of "exquisite" is overwritten by the homophonous "quiche," fusing the language of culinary refinement with that of a specific, faintly comical baked dish. Onscreen formula: "Exquisite + Quiche = Exquichite."

parts.forms: comparative more exquichite; superlative most exquichite

senses.def: Of food or drink: of the very highest delicacy and refinement; exquisite to the palate. Used as an emphatic term of culinary praise.

examples.text: I got some takeout from a Chinestaurant, and the shrimp was exquichite.

subsenses.def: Broadly, of anything: exceptionally fine, elegant, or beautifully made.

etymology: A blend of false + brainwashed + saga, as shown on screen. The compound fuses the falsity of a claim (false), the implication that its holder has been deluded or indoctrinated into it (brainwashed), and the wearying, drawn-out quality of having to endure it (saga), yielding a noun for a sustained piece of confidently mistaken nonsense.

senses.def: a statement or claim that is plainly false and misinformed, typically delivered with unearned confidence by someone who appears to have been thoroughly misled; spurious nonsense passed off as fact.

examples.text: I don't want your Falasha.
That Falasha was the second worst thing I've ever heard.

subsenses.def: the body of such misinformation considered collectively; a sustained stream of confidently wrong assertions.

example.text: I don't want your Falasha.

subsenses.def: (attributively) of, relating to, or characterized by confident misinformation.

example.text: He treated me to a full Falasha lecture about the gym.

senses.def: a single instance of such misinformed nonsense, regarded as an offence against the truth.

examples.text: That Falasha was the second worst thing I've ever heard.

senses.def: to feed someone confident misinformation; to assert something plainly false as though it were established fact.

examples.text: Stop Falasha-ing me about how the gym works.

etymology: A blend (wombo) of feel + lame + awful, telescoped into a single word so that the speaker simultaneously announces the act of feeling, the quality of being lame, and the quality of being awful. The terminal -aful echoes the homophonous snack falafel, a coincidence Professor Sendy leaves unremarked. Coined by Professor Sendy.

parts.label: informal
parts.forms: comparative more felaful; superlative most felaful

senses.def: Feeling acutely guilty about something one has said or done; overcome by a remorse so pronounced that it is felt at once as lame and as awful.

examples.text: Why am I walking so far ahead of you? I forgot you're slow. I felaful.

subsenses.def: Of a person: weighed down by self-reproach, esp. over a minor or thoughtless slight.

example.text: I felt so felaful for leaving without saying goodbye.

subsenses.def: Of conduct, a remark, or a situation: such as to provoke this guilt; cringe-inducing and regrettable.

example.text: Realizing I'd ignored her text for a week was a genuinely felaful moment.

parts.label: informal
parts.forms: third person singular present felafuls; present participle felafuling; past and past participle felafuled

senses.def: To experience this guilty, lame-and-awful feeling; to be stricken with remorse. Often used absolutely.

examples.text: I felaful.
And I don't felaful.

senses.def: The state or sensation of being felaful; a pang of self-reproach mingled with embarrassment.

examples.text: A wave of felaful washed over me the second the words left my mouth.

The first IPA reflects the on-screen tripartite stress (FEL-uh-ful); the second is the falafel-homophone realization. The same video showcases adjacent coinages and an unrelated merch plug (the "Lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura" shirt), but the defined wombo is unambiguously Felaful.

etymology: Blend of FRIED + TOASTY + PEAK + CINEMA + ANALYSIS, as shown on screen. The closing element resolves the established Sendy compound PEANEMIS (peak + cinema + analysis), a top-tier critical breakdown, to which the warming intensifiers FRIED and TOASTY are prefixed. Coined by Professor Sendy (2026). Contrast SPICYPEANEMIS (spine-chilling + icy + peak + cinema + analysis), its thermally inverted counterpart.

senses.def: a piece of film or cultural criticism delivered with conspicuous warmth and enthusiasm; a top-tier, fully cooked analysis of peak cinema that runs hot rather than cold.

examples.text: I thought he was gonna deliver a spicy one, but instead he delivered a froastypeanemis.

subsenses.def: specifically, such an analysis characterized as hot or warm in temperament, as opposed to a coldly clinical take.

example.text: The best part is that a froastypeanemis is hot, while a spicypeanemis is cold.

subsenses.def: by extension, any commentary served up unexpectedly warm and generous when a sharper or chillier one was anticipated.

example.text: It was so warm out that people were delivering froastypeanemes in the Arby's drive-through.

notes: On-screen spelling "Froastypeanemis" (black box) is authoritative; auto-captions render it as both "froastypeanemis" and the contracted "froastpeanemis," the latter recorded as a variant. On-screen etymology blue box reads exactly "Fried + Toasty + Peak + Cinema + Analysis." Core meaning (a warm/hot peak-cinema analysis) is taken directly from Sendy's captions, including his explicit hot-vs-cold contrast with SPICYPEANEMIS. Enrichment flagged as house-style: the plural "froastypeanemes," the "rare/shortened" form labels, and the IPA are editorial extrapolations, not spoken by Sendy. The same video also coins (but does not center) nonchalashouts, Sixorseveno'clockalypse, Guyknowballogist, Kirkplexed, Kirkfused, and Kirkumcised; this entry treats only the titular wombo Froastypeanemis.

etymology: A wombo formed by interleaving "genuinely" with itself — "genuinely" + "genuinely" telescoped so that the second copy is nested inside the first (gen-en-uine-uine-ly), yielding a self-reinforcing, doubled form. The video then demonstrates further accretions: an inner infix of "Kirk" produces "Genenkirkuineuinely" (genuinely + Kirk + genuinely), and the base word is compounded with the maximal stack-word "Lababybixsevenuineluperakirkilisticdeadamentalflow" to yield "Genenuineuinelababybixsevenuineluperakirkilisticdeadamentalflow." From English "genuinely" (truly, sincerely), itself from Latin "genuinus" (natural, innate).

senses.def: genuinely raised to a higher degree by nesting the word inside itself; truly and then some — used to assert sincerity with redoubled, recursive emphasis, as though one ordinary 'genuinely' were insufficient.

examples.text: I am genenuineuinely telling you, this is the realest thing I have ever said.

subsenses.def: with an internal 'Kirk' infix, signalling that the sincerity has been further escalated and brand-tagged: Genenkirkuineuinely.

example.text: That is not just true, that is genenkirkuineuinely true.

subsenses.def: in its maximal compounded form, fused with the stack-word Lababybixsevenuineluperakirkilisticdeadamentalflow to express the absolute superlative of sincerity: Genenuineuinelababybixsevenuineluperakirkilisticdeadamentalflow.

example.text: I mean it genenuineuinelababybixsevenuineluperakirkilisticdeadamentalflow, with my whole chest.

senses.def: of a feeling, statement, or person: doubly and self-evidently genuine; sincere to a recursive degree.

examples.text: His reaction was genenuineuine, the kind of real you cannot fake twice.

notes: No spoken-caption SRT exists for this video, so the definition is reconstructed from the on-screen etymology boxes (read from frame f02) and the post description, which together give the four headword forms and their root breakdowns. Confidence set to medium rather than high because the precise meaning Sendy narrates aloud could not be confirmed; the sense ('genuinely' raised to a higher power by self-nesting) is inferred directly from the (genuinely + genuinely) etymology box and is the only reading consistent with the construction. The video is structured as an escalation series: the base wombo, a Kirk-infixed variant, and two maximal compounds — these are recorded as sub-senses and a derived form. The Kirk element references the recurring 'Kirk'/'LOWKIRK' bit promoted in the same post (LOWKIRK shirt). The IPA, the part-of-speech labels, the adjective back-formation (Genenuineuine), and the gloss wording are house-style enrichment consistent with the construction, not verbatim from Sendy.

etymology: A nonce maximal-fusion wombo coined by Professor Sendy, formed by overlapping ten consecutive items of contemporary internet slang and stitching their elided syllables into a single continuous word: Genuinely + Geese In The Walmart Parking Lot + Aura + Kirk + Trippin + Caught in 4K + Break In The Door + Deadass + 4th Dimensional + Unccore. Each successive term sheds its leading and trailing sounds where they abut its neighbours (Genu-ees-italmar-parkinglot-aura-kir-rippin-in4-reakinthedoor-deada-mensional-core), producing a portmanteau in which no single boundary is cleanly recoverable.

senses.def: A maximal compound wombo produced by fusing a chain of ten distinct slang expressions into one unbroken word, in which the end of each term is elided into the beginning of the next; the chain runs Genuinely, Geese In The Walmart Parking Lot, Aura, Kirk, Trippin, Caught in 4K, Break In The Door, Deadass, 4th Dimensional, and Unccore.

notes: This is one of Professor Sendy's "mega-wombo" / chain-wombo entries rather than a single coined word with a clean two-root etymology. etymology: A productive combining element coined by Professor Sendy, derived from the adverb "genuinely" (ultimately from Latin "genuinus," natural or innate). In Sendy's system the word is reanalyzed not as a free-standing adverb but as a fusional terminal element that absorbs a preceding word or chain of words into itself, the seam typically falling on the shared or elidable sounds (as in "lowkey" + "genuinely" yielding "lowkenuinely"). The video presents it as the unifying root of a whole family of compounds.

parts.label: Sendyan
parts.forms: -genuinely; also fused as -nuinely, -uinely, -uienly after a preceding root

senses.def: A terminal combining element that fuses to the end of a word, name, or stacked phrase to signify earnest emphasis, as if to insist that the preceding sentiment is sincerely meant; the productive root of the "genuinely" wombo family.

examples.text: Lowkey plus genuinely gives you lowkenuinely.
Genuinely plus genuinely gives you genenuineuinely.

subsenses.def: Used after a single root to intensify it.

subsenses.def: Used after a stacked chain of two or more roots, the whole train collapsing onto the terminal element.

example.text: Labubu plus Baby Boo plus Burger plus genuinely gives you lababybooburgenuinely.

senses.def: Any individual coinage formed by attaching this element to a preceding word or chain; a member of the "genuinely" wombo family catalogued by Professor Sendy.

examples.text: Sixsevenuinely and sendityboouinely are both must-know genuinely wombos.
Hot plus awkward plus twin plus bruh plus genuinely gives you hawktwuenuinely.

The on-screen black title box reads "MUST KNOW GENUINELY WOMBOS" and the blue (Root + Root) boxes list nine constructions: Lowkey+Genuinely=Lowkenuinely; Lowkey+Kirk+Genuinely=Lowkirkenuinely; Labubu+Baby Boo+Genuinely=Lababyboouienly; Labubu+Baby Boo+Burger+Genuinely=Lababybooburgenuinely; Genuinely+Genuinely=Genenuineuinely; 67+Genuinely=Sixsevenuinely; Sendy+City Boy+Baby Boo+Genuinely=Sendityboouinely; Kurt Cobain+Genuinely=Kurtcobenuinely; Hot+Awkward+Twin+Bruh+Genuinely=Hawktwuenuinely. Because there is no single headword, I made the entry the shared productive element "-genuinely" itself, which is the actual subject. Sendy does not give a prose gloss of the meaning; he only recites the build-ups, so the sense ("earnest/sincere emphasis") is inferred from the base word "genuinely" and flagged here as light house-style enrichment. The IPA, the "Sendyan/combining form" labels, and the noun part-of-speech are also house-style additions. The component spellings (e.g. "Lababyboouienly," "Sendityboouinely") are taken verbatim from the description/frame, which differ slightly from the auto-caption spellings; frame/description spellings are treated as authoritative. etymology: Blend (wombo) of glow up (an upward transformation in appearance, status, or fortune) + boujee (slang variant of bourgeois, denoting conspicuous luxury or aspirational wealth). Coined by Professor Sendy. The medial -ou- is carried over from "boujee," yielding the spelling Gloujee in preference to a more phonetic *gloojee.

senses.def: Of a person, possession, or circumstance: already conspicuously luxurious yet continuing to grow more so; describing a state of wealth or refinement that is actively escalating beyond an already elevated baseline.

examples.text: I'm so gloujee that whenever I have a nonchalant crashout, it's always on a Turkish carpet.

subsenses.def: Used predicatively of a person who is both visibly improving and ostentatiously affluent.

example.text: I was already ballin', but I keep glowing up, and now I'm getting ridiculously gloujee.

senses.def: To undergo an ongoing upward transformation in luxury or status; to become ever more boujee while glowing up, especially as a deliberate act of self-elevation.

examples.text: I'm gonna get gloujee and upgrade my ticket from business class to first.

notes: On-screen headword spelling is "Gloujee" (authoritative; auto-captions render it variously as "Gloujee"/"Gloujee"). Etymology box on frame reads "Glow Up + Boujee". Sendy's stated definition: use it when something is already boujee but keeps getting boujier. The adjective sense is what he literally demonstrates ("I'm so Gloujee that...", "getting ridiculously gloujee"); the verb sense ("get gloujee") is also directly attested in his first example. House-style enrichment beyond Sendy's literal words: the comparative form "gloujier" (modeled on his spoken "boujier"), the dual POS split, the sense/subsense structure, IPA, and synonyms. Examples are lightly cleaned from his captions, with his nested wombos (e.g. "Rizzness class," "twousins," "nonchalant crashout," "Turkish carpet") normalized to plain English so the GLOUJEE entry reads cleanly. Two IPA variants given to reflect the ambiguous -ou- (long-/uː/ vs diphthong /oʊ/). Year 2026 from upload_date 20260410.

etymology: Blend of glow up (a marked positive transformation in appearance or quality) and bussin (slang: excellent, delicious). Coined by Professor Sendy, 2026. The headword fuses the two roots into a single adjective describing the result of a transformation, the "glow-up" supplying the before-and-after arc and "bussin" the emphatic verdict of approval at the end. Extended by Sendy into the noun GLUSSINADO (glussin + tornado, suggesting a sweeping, whirlwind transformation) and the verb GLUSSINADOMAXXING (glussinado + the intensifying suffix -maxx, after looksmaxxing), denoting the active, deliberate pursuit of such a turnaround.

senses.def: Of food, an object, or an experience: turning out to be excellent or delicious despite an unpromising start; markedly improved from a poor first impression to a final state worthy of emphatic approval.

examples.text: Glow up, bussin, glussin -- that food was glussin: when I first tried it it was not good, but by the time I finished it it was amazing.

subsenses.def: Specifically of something whose quality reveals itself only over the course of consumption or use, the verdict being delivered retrospectively once the transformation is complete.

example.text: Skeptical at the first bite, he declared the whole dish glussin once the plate was clean.

senses.def: A person or thing that undergoes a sudden, sweeping glow-up; one that enters in poor or unremarkable condition and emerges dramatically improved, as if transformed in a single whirlwind.

examples.text: That dude at TJ Maxx was a straight-up glussinado -- when he walked in he did not look good, but when he came out he looked so fly.

subsenses.def: By extension, the transformation itself; a glow-up of such speed or completeness that it resembles a tornado tearing through a person's appearance or a thing's quality.

example.text: The makeover was a total glussinado.

parts.label: intransitive; chiefly in the present participle glussinadomaxxing

senses.def: To pursue a glow-up actively and to the fullest extent; to dedicate oneself to engineering a dramatic before-and-after transformation, especially at a venue or occasion suited to the purpose.

examples.text: I heard you were glussinadomaxxing at the banquarterzip -- I lowkenuinely wanna go to one.

notes: On-screen black-box headword is authoritative: "Glussin" (Glow Up + Bussin), with derived forms "Glussinado" and "Glussinadomaxxing" shown in their own boxes. Senses are paraphrased from Sendy's spoken meaning: a thing/person that is unimpressive at first but ends up amazing after a transformation (his food example, his TJ Maxx-shopper example). Examples are his actual usage, lightly cleaned. ENRICHMENT added beyond what Sendy literally said: usage labels ("often predicative", "informal"), the IPA, the explicit gloss of -maxx as the looksmaxxing suffix, and the related-form note tying glussinado to the tornado/whirlwind image — all consistent with his on-screen etymology and meaning. The caption's "banquarterzip" and "lowkenuinely" are separate wombos not defined here; "banquarterzip" is retained only inside his verb example.

etymology: A chain compound (a "wombo of wombos") fusing several festival-and-slang elements into a single superlative: glussinado (a blend of glow-up + the nickname Glossinado, evoking a tornado-grade transformation) + (q)uarter zip (the half-zip pullover, emblem of casual festival drip) + (Lolla)palooza + (Coa)chella (the two marquee music festivals) + moggin (slang for decisively out-performing or "mogging" a rival) + rizz (charm; aphetic of charisma). The internal -ou- joint preserves the slurred run-on of the spoken form. Coined by Professor Sendy (2026).

senses.def: The peak grade of charisma attained only after a chain of festival-circuit triumphs: having decisively out-performed a rival at Coachella, attended Lollapalooza in a quarter zip, and undergone a glow-up so dramatic that it earns one a personal nickname.

After you've already been to Lollapalooza wearing a quarter zip, that's the glussinadouarterzipaloozachellamogginrizz.

subsenses.def: The compounding, festival-by-festival accumulation of clout, such that each event attended raises one's standing for the next.

subsenses.def: By extension, the social standing of one whose transformation is so total that friends bestow a dedicated nickname.

senses.def: A person who embodies this charisma; the festival-hardened operator who out-mogs all comers.

examples.text: Standing there in his quarter zip, he was a walking glussinadouarterzipaloozachellamogginrizz.

notes: On-screen headword spelling (identical across all six frames) is authoritative: 'glussinadouarterzipaloozachellamogginrizz'. Examples are drawn directly from his spoken captions (Coachella out-performance, Lollapalooza quarter zip, glow-up 'so good it was bussin', the nickname 'the Glossinado'), lightly cleaned. Year 2026 from upload_date 20260118.

etymology: A wombo (blend) coined by Professor Sendy, fusing GLUTTONOUS + RIGHTEOUS + MAXIMUM + FOCUS, voiced through the anatomical Latin gluteus maximus (the largest muscle of the buttock). The pseudo-Latinate frame lends the term a spurious clinical gravity, casting peak concentration as a named bodily faculty. Compare the inflected play forms demonstrated by the coiner: the intensive "extra gluteous" (Gluttonous + Righteous) and its jocular antonymic foil "gluten free," together with the verbal "gluteousmaxximaxxing" (under the influence of the -maxxing suffix).

etymology: A compound of GOAT (acronym, "Greatest Of All Time") + peanemis. The first element fuses the four words Greatest + Of + All + Time, while the second element, peanemis, is itself a prior Sendy compound of Peak + Cinema + Analysis, denoting elite film criticism at its highest pitch. The seam between the two is elided so that the terminal -t of "Time" runs straight into "peanemis," yielding Goatpeanemis. Compare the related back-of-house form Behindthesceanemis (Behind + the + scenes + peanemis).

of the very highest order of film analysis; constituting peak cinema criticism that is also the greatest of all time.

notes: COMPILATION VIDEO, not a single-word definition reel. etymology: A chain-portmanteau (a "mega-wombo") assembled by telescoping six smaller wombos end to end: Gooney (George Clooney) + Janiston (Jennifer Aniston) + Adandler (Adam Sandler) + Twift (Taylor Swift) + Sabrarpenter (Sabrina Carpenter) + pizzarties (pizza parties). Each component is itself a Sendy blend in which a name or phrase is crushed to a single fused token; the components are then overlapped at their seams (…goon- + -ist- + -and- + -ift- + -rarpenter + -pizzarties) to yield one continuous word. The blue-box roots shown on screen are the six fully spelled source items.

senses.def: A single fused word formed by chaining together several distinct wombos one after another, such that an unbroken string of celebrity names and a closing phrase reads as one continuous utterance; the canonical specimen blending George Clooney, Jennifer Aniston, Adam Sandler, Taylor Swift, Sabrina Carpenter, and pizza parties.

examples.text: George Clooney, Sabrina Carpenter, Jennifer Aniston, Adam Sandler, Taylor Swift, pizza parties — Goonistandiftrarpenterpizzarties.

subsenses.def: By extension, any maximally compressed run-on built from multiple smaller blends spoken in immediate succession.

etymology: A wombo blend of "guy" + "knows" + "ball" + the learned suffix "-ologist." On the model of the mock-academic title (compare gynecologist, from which Sendy derives the form by substituting "guy knows ball" for the opening syllables), it dresses casual expertise in the costume of a credentialed specialist. The element "knows ball" draws on the colloquial sense of "to know ball" — to possess genuine, demonstrable competence in a domain — while "-ologist" lends the spurious authority of a degree-holding professional.

senses.def: A person who possesses a great deal of knowledge about a particular subject; one regarded, half-seriously, as a credentialed expert in some field of interest.

examples.text: Use this wombo when someone has a lot of knowledge about something.
I went to my guyknowballogist and he explained that I've been overdoing it.

subsenses.def: An informally consulted authority treated as a go-to source of explanation or diagnosis, much as one would visit a specialist.

example.text: When I needed answers I booked a session with my guyknowballogist.

subsenses.def: By extension, a trusted, in-the-know companion who provides knowledgeable counsel; a confidant of the well-informed sort.

example.text: A guyknowballogist is often your supportive bro, sometimes your chill bestie, and likely your most dependable fella.

senses.def: To hold forth knowledgeably on a subject; to explain or expound in the manner of a self-styled expert.

examples.text: He guyknowballogized about it for an hour before I could get a word in.

notes: Headword spelling and etymology taken authoritatively from the on-screen black box ("Guyknowballogist") and blue root boxes ("Guy + Knows + Ball + ologist"); auto-captions render it variously as "guyknowballogist" and as the literal "guy / knows / ball and / -ologist." Sendy builds the word aloud from "gynecologist." The core sense (a person with deep knowledge of a subject) is exactly what he states. Sense 2 (the trusted-confidant shading) is a tasteful house-style extension grounded in his own follow-up that a guyknowballogist is "often your glazed bronut... sometimes your pell bepper... likely your unbrella" — i.e. a supportive, in-the-know companion figure. The verb part of speech is original house enrichment (no verb form is literally demonstrated) but is consistent with the agentive "-ologist" pattern; flagged here. The clip is dense with nested wombos used in the example sentences (quiche loreain, Vagynyamaxxing, brarpetorium, glazed bronut, pell bepper, unbrella, sope opera) which are demonstrations, not the headword.

etymology: Blend (wombo) of guy + knows + ball + the combining form -ology (from Greek -logia "study of, science of"). Coined by Professor Sendy, who parses the word on screen as "Guy + Knows + Ball + ology." The terminal -ology lends the coinage the mock register of an academic discipline, framing a guy's accumulated knowledge as a formal field of study.

senses.def: The mock-academic discipline concerned with the body of knowledge a guy possesses about a given subject; the study or science of what a guy knows about something.

examples.text: Twuh is majoring in English, but his actual major is in guyknowballogy.
He studied guyknowballogy in brollege and is now quite successful.

subsenses.def: As an academic course of study; a major or field one can pursue at college.

example.text: His actual major is in guyknowballogy.

subsenses.def: By extension, the practical mastery or expertise a guy has accumulated in some pursuit, treated as if it were a credentialed science.

example.text: He has 6.7 mansions because he studied guyknowballogy.

notes: On-screen headword and etymology boxes read "Guyknowballogy" and "Guy + Knows + Ball + ology"; this spelling is authoritative over the auto-caption rendering. Sendy's spoken gloss: "study the science a guy knows knowledge in something," rewritten here into dictionary prose. His two usage examples ("twuh is majoring in English... actual major is in guyknowballogy" and "underminunbrella studied guyknowballogy in brollege and now is quite successful... 6.7 mansions") are preserved as the example sentences and underpin sub-senses 1a (as a college major/field) and 1b (as expertise yielding success/wealth). The examples are densely packed with other Sendy wombos (twuh, quiche, loreains, rendezboos, wombanizer, underminunbrella, brollege, susessful) which are tangential and not defined here. The IPA is a plausible house-style reconstruction stressing the -ology pattern (cf. biology); part of speech (noun) and the usage label "humorous" are house enrichment consistent with the coinage's mock-academic framing.

etymology: A serial blend layering five common internet interjections and intensifiers, articulated as a single breathless word: HAWK (from hot, by way of the "hawk tuah" register) + (a)WK(ward) + TW(in) + (br)U(h) + (g)ENUINELY. Each subsequent form is generated by suffixing one further root to the running chain: + SERENDIPITY yields the medial variant, and + AKIMBO the terminal one. The construction belongs to Professor Sendy's "stacking" school of wombo formation, in which the joke resides in the accretion itself rather than in any settled sense.

senses.def: used as an all-purpose intensifier collapsing several stock reactions into one utterance; in earnest truth, and with maximum sincerity, awkwardness, and emphasis all at once.

subsenses.def: as a sincerity marker: for real; no exaggeration.

example.text: Hot, awkward, twin, bruh -- genuinely. Hawktwuenuinely.

subsenses.def: as an exclamation of flustered admiration directed at an attractive or striking person or thing.

example.text: He is, hawktwuenuinely, the most beautiful man I have ever seen.

examples.text: Hawktwuenuinely, I have never been more serious in my life.

senses.def: in its extended forms, the same intensifier raised by degrees, each appended root marking a further escalation: with the addition of serendipity (Hawktwuenuinelerendipity) denoting that the sincere astonishment is also a happy accident, and with the addition of akimbo (Hawktwuenuinelerendipitakimbo) denoting that it is delivered emphatically, hands on hips and elbows out.

examples.text: We met by pure chance -- hawktwuenuinelerendipity.
She stood there, hands on her hips, hawktwuenuinelerendipitakimbo.

senses.def: an exclamation expressing earnest, slightly flustered enthusiasm; rendered as a single chained outburst rather than the five separate reactions it contains.

examples.text: Hawktwuenuinely!

notes: The on-screen black headword box renders the word as "Hawktwuenuienly" (with a transposed vowel cluster), but the lower-third pink badge and Sendy's own post description both give "Hawktwuenuinely"; the description spelling is treated as authoritative for all three run-on forms. Sendy speaks only the component roots in sequence (hot, awkward, twin, bruh, genuinely, serendipity, akimbo) and does not narrate an explicit meaning, so the senses below are tasteful house-style reconstructions consistent with the slang roots he chains, not definitions he literally utters; flagged accordingly. Confidence medium for this reason. The three stacked forms are entered as escalating variants of one headword. Upload date 20260305 -> year 2026. Description also promotes an unrelated "LOWKIRK / The Lou" merch shirt (Etsy STLBrendonStore), which is incidental to the wombo.

etymology: A phonetic mondegreen-wombo coined by Professor Sendy, formed by collapsing the syllables of a rapidly spoken everyday utterance into a single orthographic word. The string resolves on the ear to a slurred greeting and follow-up question of the order "Hi, [name] — can you, uh, want some... ?", with the run-on tail (-ontchalaura) preserving the trailing mumble of an unfinished offer. Compare Sendy's broader practice of transcribing fast, casual speech as one undivided word.

senses.def: A single-word rendering of a hurried, half-swallowed spoken greeting and follow-up offer, transcribed exactly as it lands on the ear rather than as it would be properly spelled; the verbal blur of someone running a salutation and a question together without pause.

subsenses.def: Used to capture the moment of casual social mumble in which the individual words of a familiar phrase become acoustically inseparable.

examples.text: He breezed past me with a cheerful "highrekenuolontchalaura" and was gone before I could answer.

parts.label: slang

example.text: I went to the Huzzitorium, but the bouncer threw me out for coming on too strong to the baby boo on the dance floor.

subsenses.def: In the paired form Bruzzitorium, the corresponding venue stocked with men, or frequented chiefly to meet them.

examples.text: They went to the Bruzzitorium to meet men, since they only like to date kirkilistic word-comblers.

Sendy presents the two forms as a paired set differentiated by which gender of prospective partner the venue is stocked with (Huzzitorium = women/huzz, Bruzzitorium = men/bruzz); this gendered split is treated as the two numbered senses and is explicit in his examples (the Huzzitorium with a dance floor and bouncer; the Bruzzitorium frequented by men). The auto-caption 'me out for Vagynya' is garbled; the example sentence has been lightly reconstructed to preserve his intent without inventing new content. The added noun part of speech for 'Bruzzitorium' as a co-headword and the synonym list are house-style enrichment consistent with his stated meaning. His example chains contain further nested wombos (twomantic, quiche Peanemis, cunnilinguists, kirkilistic womblers, clooked, Loving Bropp, nonchalashingout, Brinternet) which are quoted as he used them but not separately defined here.

etymology: Coined by Professor Sendy (2026) as a "mesoperiodic" portmanteau telescoping the names of the first twenty chemical elements of the periodic table into a single word, each element's name overlapping or eliding into the next: hydrogen + helium + lithium + beryllium + boron + carbon + nitrogen + oxygen + fluorine + neon + sodium + magnesium + aluminum + silicon + phosphorus + sulfur + chlorine + argon + potassium + calcium. The prefix element "meso-" (Greek mesos, "middle") in Sendy's framing denotes a hybrid table in which the discrete cells of the conventional periodic chart are fused into one continuous string.

senses.def: A single continuous word formed by fusing the names of the first twenty elements of the periodic table in atomic-number order, each name run together with and partially elided into the one following; the verbal collapse of an ordered series of element names into one unbroken term.

examples.text: Behold the Mesoperiodic Table of Elements: Hydreliumlithylliumboronarbonitroxygenfluoronsosiumaluiliconphosphorulfurchlargonpotium.

subsenses.def: Specifically, the telescoped run from hydrogen through calcium, in which adjacent element names share or drop letters at their seams (as in hydrogen + helium yielding 'hydrelium', or chlorine + argon yielding 'chlargon').

example.text: 'Chlargon' is where chlorine and argon meet in the mesoperiodic string.

subsenses.def: By extension, any practice of reciting an ordered scientific or numerical sequence as one elided, breathless portmanteau rather than as discrete terms.

example.text: He read the whole list mesoperiodically, in one breath, with no spaces.

senses.def: Of or relating to the fused, gapless form of the periodic table in which the separate cells of element names are merged into a single uninterrupted word.

examples.text: On the mesoperiodic table the elements are not arranged in cells but melted into one word.

notes: No SRT captions exist for this video, so the definition is reconstructed from the on-screen text boxes (the headword, the "Mesoperiodic Table of Elements" title, and the etymology box listing the 20 source elements) plus the post description, rather than from spoken audio; hence confidence is medium. On-screen spelling of the headword is taken as authoritative and reproduced exactly. The 20 source roots are read directly from the blue etymology box (hydrogen through calcium). The 'mesoperiodic' adjective and the noun's sub-sense (b) ('reciting any ordered series as one elided portmanteau') are tasteful house-style enrichments consistent with Sendy's evident meaning, not phrases he literally speaks; the title's adjective 'Mesoperiodic' is his and is the basis for the derived adjective entry. IPA is an approximate, plausible rendering of the full telescoped word in the dry house style and is necessarily a best-effort reconstruction given the word's invented seams. The description repeats the headword with a minor variant spelling ('aluiliconphosphorulfurchlargonpotium' vs. on-screen line break); the frame's spelling was preferred.

etymology: A wombo blend of "smart" and the internet slang phrase "I know ball" (an expression of competence or expertise, especially regarding sports or, more broadly, of "getting it"). Coined by Professor Sendy. The construction is doubly punning: the sense of "smart" is folded into "I know ball," whose final word "ball" is in turn deformed toward "small," yielding a phrase whose surface meaning ("I know small," i.e. I know little) is the comic inversion of the cleverness the parent words imply.

parts.label: slang
parts.forms: also styled I Know Small; pl. unchanged

senses.def: A self-deprecating declaration that one possesses little knowledge, skill, or understanding of a subject; the deadpan inverse of the boast "I know ball."

examples.text: Whenever you quiche, loreain, the peanemis, it's really huge but kinda dumb; but whenever I do, I know small.

subsenses.def: Said in modest or rueful contrast to another who claims competence; an admission of being out of one's depth.

example.text: You really know your cinema; me, I know small.

subsenses.def: As an interjection: a flat, comic concession of ignorance offered in place of an answer.

example.text: Asked to explain the lore, he simply shrugged and said, "I know small."

parts.label: in the phrase to know small
parts.forms: knows small; knew small; knowing small

senses.def: To understand or be skilled in something to a negligible degree; to lack expertise while affecting none.

examples.text: I know small, but I'll watch you do the peanemis anyway.

notes: Sourced from a Professor Sendy musical compilation that strings together four headline wombos (I Know Small, Larpet, Rendezboo, Shiggles); "I Know Small" leads the title and the on-screen frame, so it is treated as the primary headword. On-screen etymology box reads exactly "Smart + I Know Ball = I Know Small." The captioned lyric contrasts the addressee ("whenever YOU... it's really huge but kinda dumb") with the speaker ("whenever I... I know small"), establishing the sense as a deadpan profession of one's own limited knowledge or competence, inverting the boast "I know ball." The interjectional sense (b) and the related verb phrase ("to know small") are tasteful house-style enrichments extrapolated from his usage, not separately defined by Sendy. The lyric also embeds further unrelated wombos in passing (quiche = quirky + niche; loreain = lore + explain; peanemis = peak + cinema + analysis), which are noted only as context and not defined here. IPA is a plausible dry-register rendering. Confidence medium because the video bundles multiple coinages and Sendy's gloss is delivered as song lyric rather than a formal single-word definition.

etymology: Initialism of in my honest goonion, a humorous variant of the established internet abbreviation IMHO ("in my honest opinion"). The final element substitutes goonion, a blend of good and opinion, for the conventional opinion, yielding a phrase that explicitly marks the stated view as a favorable one. Coined by Professor Sendy, who pairs it with the antonymic form IMHB ("in my honest bunnion," from bunk + opinion) for unfavorable views.

senses.def: Used to preface a statement as a favorable or approving personal view; an emphatically positive counterpart to IMHO, signaling that the opinion being offered is a good one.

examples.text: IMHG, I think he's really a vagynya-maxxer who drops falasha 67 times a day, 41 days a year.

subsenses.def: Employed when one wishes to broadcast a flattering or generous opinion widely, especially across social media.

example.text: Use this online slang when you wanna spread your good opinion all over the internet.

senses.def: A favorable opinion; a view held or expressed in a positive, approving spirit.

examples.text: In my honest goonion, that was the best thing he's ever done.

subsenses.def: Contrasted with bunnion (a poor or worthless opinion); when one's goonion isn't any good, it is downgraded to a bunnion.

example.text: So if your goonion isn't so good, let's say it's bunk, then it's a bunnion.

notes: On-screen text confirms the headword spelling IMHG and the gloss In My Honest Goonion; the blue etymology box gives Goonion = Good + Opinion. Definitions paraphrased from Sendy's captions (he frames it as online slang for spreading a good opinion across the internet) rather than copied. Sense 2 (goonion as a standalone noun) and the IMHB / bunnion antonym (Bunk + Opinion) are drawn directly from his spoken examples, where a low-quality view becomes a bunnion. House-style enrichment: the part-of-speech split (interjection/sentence adverb vs. the noun goonion), the usage labels, and the IPA are original framing consistent with his meaning. Example sentences are lightly cleaned from his actual captions; the surrounding gag vocabulary (vagynya-maxxing, falasha, etc.) is preserved as he delivered it.

The collarbone element imports the vocabulary of looksmaxxing (the deliberate optimization of one's physical appearance), in which a pronounced clavicle is prized; thus the compound fuses the language of romantic attachment with that of facial- and frame-aesthetic culture.

etymology: Blend of jo(b) + applications, formed by overlaying the initial "j-" of "job" onto the standard plural "applications" so that a single syllable does the work of two words. A viewer-coinage surfaced and popularized by Professor Sendy, who presented it as the third comment on his "lowkirkentological lowstate" video, where it had drawn some 372,000 likes. Sendy notes, with scholarly caution, that the resulting onset "Jap-" coincides with a recognized ethnic slur, an unintended phonetic collision rather than a feature of the blend's meaning.

senses.def: Job applications, esp. considered collectively as the tedious, repetitive paperwork of seeking employment; the formal submissions one files to be considered for a position.

subsenses.def: A single such submission; one entry in the broader campaign of seeking work.

example.text: She fired off six japplications before lunch and heard back from none.

subsenses.def: By extension, the wider ordeal or grind of job-hunting; the act of applying as a sustained activity.

example.text: Another weekend lost to the japplications.

examples.text: The third comment suggested that job applications should now be called japplications, and it had 372,000 likes.
If you all go out there and use that word, just be careful.

etymology: A blend (wombo) of jealous and nonchalant. Jealous derives from Middle English jelous, from Old French jalous, from Late Latin zēlōsus ("full of zeal, ardent"), itself from Latin zēlus ("zeal, jealousy"), borrowed from Greek zêlos. Nonchalant comes from French nonchalant, the present participle of nonchaloir ("to lack warmth, be indifferent"), from non- ("not") + chaloir ("to care, concern oneself"), ultimately from Latin calēre ("to be warm"). The compound captures the paradox of an otherwise unbothered disposition pierced by a single, localized flare of jealousy. Coined by Professor Sendy.

parts.label: informal
parts.forms: jealonchalant; comparative more jealonchalant; superlative most jealonchalant

senses.def: Of a person: ordinarily secure, carefree, and free of jealousy within a relationship, yet given to a sudden, narrowly focused pang of possessiveness when in the presence of one particular individual.

examples.text: Maybe you're jealonchalant: completely secure in your relationship, never the jealous type, until that one person walks in.

subsenses.def: Characterized by a default state of nonchalance: untroubled, carefree, and indifferent to ordinary causes of romantic insecurity.

example.text: He's usually so jealonchalant that nothing fazes him about who she talks to.

subsenses.def: Designating the brief twinge of jealousy that surfaces only around a single trigger person, breaking through an otherwise placid composure.

example.text: She felt that jealonchalant twinge the moment his ex appeared, though she's never jealous otherwise.

parts.label: informal; the quality denoted by the adjective

senses.def: The condition of being jealonchalant; a generally unbothered, secure temperament punctuated by occasional, person-specific jealousy.

examples.text: His jealonchalance only shows up around that one coworker; the rest of the time he doesn't worry about anything.

notes: On-screen headword "Jealonchalant" (black box) with etymology "Jealous + Nonchalant" (blue boxes) is authoritative and matches the SRT and the post description exactly; confidence high. Definition is paraphrased from Sendy's captions in house style: secure/carefree/never jealous by default, with a localized twinge of jealousy around "one particular person." Enrichment beyond what Sendy literally said: (1) the sub-senses 1a/1b splitting the default nonchalance from the targeted jealousy; (2) the comparative/superlative forms; (3) the derived noun part of speech "jealonchalance" and its example — Sendy demonstrated only the adjective, so the noun form, its spelling, and its usage example are my original house-style extensions consistent with his meaning; (4) the synonyms and the second IPA variant. The IPA is a plausible house-style reconstruction, not transcribed from his pronunciation. The video's upload_date is 20260614, so firstKnownUse and all example years are 2026.

etymology: A proper name (Kirk) repurposed by Professor Sendy as an all-purpose combining element. In the Sendy idiom the syllable is not affixed in any one fixed position but spliced into a host word wherever a similar-sounding syllable already lurks, yielding a family of blends (Lowkey + Kirk + Genuinely → Lowkirkenuinely; Ridiculous + Kirk → Rikirkulous). Compare the established practice of the infix, as in the colloquial intensifier of "fan-bloody-tastic"; Kirk functions as a named, person-specific infix-cum-blend root rather than a meaning-bearing morpheme.

senses.def: A syllable derived from the name Kirk, inserted into or fused onto a host word to coin a new blend (or wombo); placed wherever the host word offers a phonetically hospitable syllable, whether at the beginning, in the middle, or at the end.

examples.text: Lowkey plus Kirk plus genuinely gives lowkirkenuinely.
Twin plus Kirk gives twirk.

subsenses.def: Inserted medially, as an infix that interrupts the host word.

example.text: Ridiculous, with Kirk worked into the middle, becomes rikirkulous.

subsenses.def: Prefixed or suffixed, attaching at the head or tail of the host word.

example.text: Kirk plus forever gives kirkever.

senses.def: Used as the productive element of an open-ended class of such coinages, the so-called Kirk wombos, a fixed set of which are presented as essential or canonical.

examples.text: The must-know Kirk wombos include lowkirkenuinely, flowkirkenuinely, kirkumcision, twirk, kirkfused, rikirkulous, kirkrisma, kurtkirkcobenuinely, and kirkever.
Kirk plus charisma gives kirkrisma.

senses.def: To splice the element Kirk into (a word), producing a wombo; to subject a word to this operation.

examples.text: To kirk podium and precision is to arrive at kirkumcision.
Confused, once kirked, becomes kirkfused.

notes: The source video (titled MUST KNOW KIRK WOMBOS, uploaded 2026-06-05) is a compilation rather than a single definition: it presents nine separate blends all built on the root Kirk. Per the one-entry brief, the headword is taken as the unifying element Kirk itself, treated as a combining form, with the nine demonstrated derivatives recorded as its attested products. Sendy never states an explicit gloss for Kirk; the senses below paraphrase the function the video demonstrates (a syllable inserted into or fused onto host words to coin new wombos). The two parts of speech (combining form; transitive verb) are house-style enrichment extrapolated from his usage — he shows the splicing operation repeatedly but does not name a verb form. The full list of attested blends from the captions/frame: Lowkirkenuinely, Flowkirkenuinely, Kirkumcision, Twirk, Kirkfused, Rikirkulous, Kirkrisma, Kurtkirkcobenuinely, Kirkever. IPA given in the dry house style for the standard pronunciation of the name. The shirt "Lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura" referenced in the description is a separate merch item, not defined here.

etymology: Blend of Kirk (the internet-folkloric figure and shorthand for outsize, aura-coded magnetism) and charisma (Greek khárisma, "favour, gift," via the sense "compelling personal allure"). Coined by Professor Sendy in 2026. The portmanteau elides the shared medial -k-/c- and the -risma of charisma, yielding a single fused noun. Compare the related humorous derivative kirkfused (Kirk + confused), used to describe the cognitive overwhelm produced by an excess of Kirkrisma.

senses.def: Personal magnetism of such overwhelming intensity that it ceases to register as ordinary charm and becomes an almost ambient force; charisma amplified to the saturation point associated with the figure "Kirk."

examples.text: He has Kirkrisma.
His aura has gotten so large I literally can't feel it anymore.

subsenses.def: the aura or radiant presence itself, considered as a measurable quantity that can grow large enough to exceed perception.

example.text: His aura has gotten so large I literally can't feel it anymore.

subsenses.def: the disorienting effect such magnetism has on a bystander; the state of being kirkfused.

example.text: I'm so kirkfused.

notes: On-screen etymology box reads "Kirk + Charisma = Kirkrisma," which is authoritative for spelling. This video is a multi-wombo rapid-fire post defining four blends; per instructions I selected the lead headword (first on screen and first in the title) and documented the rest here. The companion wombos and their on-screen etymologies are: (1) Peanemis = Peak + Cinema + Analysis — a triple blend for a peak, deeply analytical take on a film; Sendy's usage references "K-pop Burger Hunters 2" not being as good as the original "but I heard it's still great," and the imperative "Give me the full peanemis." (2) Charismashout = Charisma + Crashout — an emotional breakdown so magnetic that onlookers are drawn in and want the person's autograph; usage: "She's having a Charismashout... she's getting really emotional but people are drawn to her, want to be around her, and they wanna get her autograph." (3) Peakulu = Peak + Delulu — peak delusion, esp. believing one possesses Kirkrisma when one does not; usage: "He's peakulu — he thinks he has Kirkrisma but really he's just delulu... and I'm here for it." The caption's "DeLuluLemonusional" appears to be an auto-caption garble of "delusional" and is not treated as a headword. House-style enrichment: the IPA, the formal restatement of senses/subsenses, the recording of kirkfused as a derivative, and the synonym list are my additions consistent with Sendy's stated meaning. The shirt plug ("Lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura") in the description is merch promo, not a defined entry.

etymology: Blend (wombo) of Kirk + podium + precision, after the model of circumcision. Coined by Professor Sendy (2026). The form deliberately echoes circumcision both in sound and in its underlying sense of trimming away excess, here applied to verbiage rather than flesh; the verb kirkumcise and participle kirkumcised follow by back-formation.

senses.def: A pointed demand that a person make something — typically a piece of speech, an explanation, or a presentation — more precise and direct; the act or process of cutting overlong, meandering, or padded talk down to its sharp essentials.

subsenses.def: Applied to rambling discourse: a trimming-away of excess words so that only the direct point remains.

example.text: Your quiche peanemis needs a kirkumcision when you were loreaining it to me and vacaydreaming about last summer.

subsenses.def: More generally, the quality of forced concision or sharpened directness imposed on something diffuse.

example.text: You need to tighten up the quicheness.

examples.text: Your really long Quiche Loreain needs a kirkumcision; that's my goonion and I stand by it.

senses.def: To make (something) more precise and direct by cutting away its excess; to tighten up rambling speech or content to its essentials.

examples.text: I think all of our classes need to be kirkumcised, because none of these brofessors will allow us to use any quiche wombos.

notes: On-screen headword box reads "Kirkumcision"; etymology box reads "Kirk + Podium + Precision" (spoken caption renders it "Kirk + podium + precision"). Definition reflects Sendy's actual stated meaning: a demand to make something more precise and direct, i.e. to tighten up overlong, meandering speech. The verb form (kirkumcise / kirkumcised) is drawn directly from his usage ("need to be kirkumcised"). His example sentences are studded with other wombos (quiche = quirky + niche; peanemis = peak cinema analysis; loreaining = lore + explaining; vacaydreaming = vacation + daydreaming; goonion = good + opinion; brofessors; Womboclat = wombo + bomboclat); these have been preserved and lightly glossed in the examples. House-style enrichment: the precision noun sense (1b), the explicit verb part of speech, the IPA, and the synonym list are original additions consistent with his meaning. Description hawks a "Lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura" shirt (STLBrendonStore) but the video itself is a genuine wombo definition.

etymology: A wombo blend of Kurt Cobain (the late frontman of Nirvana, b. 1967, d. 1994) and the adverb GENUINELY, the grunge icon's surname being fused into the body of the adverb so that the two are heard as a single word. The base form serves as the seed of an escalating compound chain, each link grafting a further root onto the last: Kurtcobentologiclowstate (incorporating LOWKIRK and a "low state"), Kurtcobentologiclicular (incorporating PARTICULAR), and Kurtcobentologiclicularaura (incorporating AURA). Coined by Professor Sendy.

parts.label: wombo
parts.forms: Kurtcobenuinely; escalating forms Kurtcobentologiclowstate, Kurtcobentologiclicular, Kurtcobentologiclicularaura

senses.def: Genuinely; sincerely; in a manner that is truly and authentically so. Used to affirm that something is meant in earnest, the grunge surname lending the adverb a note of weary, flannel-clad candor.

examples.text: I am Kurtcobenuinely glad you made it.

subsenses.def: (in the escalating chain) used as the seed of a progressively lengthening compound, each step appending a further root for comic emphasis, as Kurtcobentologiclowstate, Kurtcobentologiclicular, Kurtcobentologiclicularaura.

example.text: He started at Kurtcobenuinely and did not stop until Kurtcobentologiclicularaura.

senses.def: Particular; pertaining to a specific case; relating to the genuine, Cobain-tinged quality named by the base form. An adjectival extension of the chain incorporating the root PARTICULAR.

examples.text: That is a very Kurtcobentologiclicular concern of yours.

senses.def: The aura or radiant quality of the foregoing; the fully escalated terminal form of the chain, appending the root AURA to denote the sincere, particular essence as a palpable atmosphere.

examples.text: There was a certain Kurtcobentologiclicularaura about the whole room.

notes: No spoken-caption SRT exists for this video, so the definition is reconstructed from the authoritative on-screen text boxes and the post description rather than from Sendy's spoken gloss; confidence set to medium accordingly. The frame is clear and shows four stacked forms with their blue root-boxes: Kurtcobenuinely (Kurt Cobain + Genuinely), Kurtcobentologiclowstate (+ Lowkirkentologiclowstate), Kurtcobentologiclicular (+ Particular), Kurtcobentologiclicularaura (+ Aura). The headword is the foundational, shortest form; the longer forms are documented as a usage label ("in the escalating chain"). Sense divisions, the adjectival/noun extensions, and the IPA are house-style enrichment consistent with the displayed blend, since no spoken meaning was available to paraphrase. The description also promotes a "LOWKIRK shirt," which corroborates the Lowkirk root in the second form.

etymology: A chain wombo coined by Professor Sendy, assembled by phonetically welding successive roots onto a shared seam so that each new word grows out of the tail of the one before it. The base, Kurtcobenuinely, fuses Kurt Cobain + genuinely; a second pass interleaves Kirk to yield Kurtkirkobenuinely; the suffix is then repeatedly re-spliced through memento (-ento), lenticular (-enticular), and talented legends (-entedlegends), producing the maximal form Kurtkirkobenuenticulentedlegends. The construction relies throughout on the overlap of shared sounds at each join (the -en- / -ent- / -ic- pivots) rather than simple concatenation.

parts.label: chain wombo
parts.forms: also in shorter build-stages Kurtcobenuinely, Kurtkirkobenuinely, Kurtkirkobenuento, Kurtkirkobenuenticular

senses.def: The maximal form of a progressively assembled compound, built by fusing the name Kurt Cobain to a chain of further roots—Kirk, genuinely, memento, lenticular, and talented legends—each spliced onto the previous word at a shared phonetic seam.

subsenses.def: In its base stage, the simple fusion of Kurt Cobain and genuinely.

example.text: He sized up the grunge icon and pronounced him, Kurtcobenuinely, the real thing.

subsenses.def: In its extended stages, the same fusion lengthened by interleaving additional roots without breaking the seam, up to the full Kurtkirkobenuenticulentedlegends.

example.text: By the final pass it had swollen into Kurtkirkobenuenticulentedlegends, a single word holding Kirk, a memento, something lenticular, and a roster of talented legends.

notes: A chain-style wombo with no fixed lexical meaning; the entry documents the build sequence shown on screen. The final stacked word Kurtkirkobenuenticulentedlegends is the authoritative headword per the frame; the four shorter intermediate forms are recorded as synonyms/alternate build-stages. Auto-captions render the roots as Kurt Cobain, Kirk, genuinely, memento, lenticular, talented legends; on-screen blue boxes confirm these (spelling 'Genuienly' as shown is Sendy's stylized box text—standard 'genuinely' used in glosses). Examples are house-style illustrations constructed around Sendy's actual roots, since this video offers no narrative usage sentence. IPA is an approximation of the full maximal form in the dry house style. Description and title both confirm the spelling chain. Merch line in the description (the 'Lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura' shirt) is a separate gag and not part of this entry.
