etymology: Coined by Professor Sendy (2026). A compound wombo built from four roots: PEAK (slang for the very best, the height of quality) + CINEMA (in the modern sense of something so well-crafted it deserves the screen) blended into "peanema," joined with CANDID (frank, unguarded, free of flattery) + ANALYSIS (a careful examination or appraisal) blended into "canalysis." The full term thus fuses the idea of peak-cinematic excellence with that of a brutally honest appraisal.

senses.def: An assessment or take that is at once first-rate and unsparingly honest; a piece of frank, no-flattery commentary delivered with such polish or conviction that it reaches a peak, cinematic standard.

examples.text: It's time to construct a peanema canalysis.
My guy-knows-ball-ology brofessor constructed a peanema canalysis in front of the whole class.

subsenses.def: the act or instance of delivering such candid, top-tier feedback in front of others, as a performance or pronouncement.

example.text: He stood up and offered the room a peanema canalysis.

parts.label: the candid-analysis element used on its own

senses.def: A candid analysis; a frank, unvarnished appraisal of a thing or situation, offered straightforwardly and without softening.

examples.text: It's time to construct a canalysis.

senses.def: to deliver a candid analysis of; to appraise (someone or something) frankly and straightforwardly.

examples.text: Don't sugarcoat it — just canalyse the situation for me.

notes: On-screen headword and etymology are unambiguous: \"Peanema Canalysis\" = Peak + Cinema + Candid + Analysis (frame f02.jpg). The captions establish the core meaning: a wombo \"used when you deliver\" something \"really straightforward and honest.\" Sendy demonstrates the second element on its own as a noun (\"it's time to construct a canalysis\") and the full phrase as a thing one \"constructs\" in front of an audience, so I have split the entry into the standalone noun \"canalysis\" (sense set) plus the full compound term, and added a transitive verb sense (\"to canalyse / construct a canalysis\") as tasteful house-style enrichment consistent with his \"construct a canalysis\" usage. The two usage examples are lightly cleaned from his own captions. Year 2026 from upload_date 20260507. The IPA is a plausible dry-register rendering, not authoritative. The surrounding caption also strings together unrelated wombos (twusin, strutal, glazed bronuts, peans, keeshes, guyknowballogy, brofessor, chalarping, cooshout, capquishe) purely as filler examples; the defined headword for this video is Peanema Canalysis.

etymology: A blend of peak + cinema + analysis, glossed by Professor Sendy on screen as “Peanemis = Peak + Cinema + Analysis.” It is the bare nominal core of a large family of prefixed forms, in which a modifier-blend is stacked onto the front and the terminal -peanemis is never shed (e.g. mysterpeanemis, kingpeanemis, goatpeanemis).

parts.label: informal; chiefly in “deliver the ___” or “give me the ___”

senses.def: a thing held to be of the very highest order — at once excellent, of cinematic stature, and meriting serious critical appraisal; that which is supremely worth experiencing and dissecting.

subsenses.def: a take, opinion, or verdict delivered as though it were a definitive, peak-tier cinematic analysis; the elevated reading one renders on something otherwise mundane.

example.text: Bro, go to the party and please deliver the peanemis.

subsenses.def: (jocular) a dish or order so impeccably composed that it rises to the level of art; a peak culinary offering.

senses.def: the act or product of so appraising; a top-tier critical reading delivered with the gravity of film analysis.

examples.text: I haven’t seen the show — could you just give me the peanemis?

senses.def: chiefly in derived compounds: a specific grade or flavor of peanemis, formed by prefixing a modifier-blend to the stem. Documented forms include angledpeanemis (delivered through agitation), captenpeanemis (captivating in delivery), kingpeanemis (effortless, laid-back), mizzpeanemis (dominating, charismatic), loredpeanemis (lore-rich, deeply researched), supremepeanemis (of maximal intensity), quaranteanemis (of the quarantine era), mysterpeanemis (mysterious, unexplained), docderpeanemis (documented and filed), and goatpeanemis (the greatest of all time).
(of a thing or service) effortlessly attaining peanemis quality; reliably and automatically of a peak, cinematic, analysis-worthy standard.

examples.text: The whole experience was completely peanematic — peak from start to finish.

etymology: A wombo (blended coinage) formed from PEAK (internet slang for the highest attainable quality; the best, the apex) + GENIUS, the two telescoped at their shared medial syllable so that the -eak of "peak" elides into the gen- of "genius," yielding Pea- + -nius. The construction preserves the superlative force of "peak" while grafting on the intellectual prestige of "genius," producing a single term that asserts both excellence and brilliance at once. First recorded in the usage of Professor Sendy.

senses.def: a person or thing regarded as simultaneously the finest of its kind and conspicuously brilliant; an apex of both quality and intelligence.

examples.text: Peak plus genius equals peanius.

subsenses.def: a stroke of inspired thinking so good that ordinary praise will not suffice; a top-tier idea or move.

example.text: Putting the wombos on a shirt was straight-up peanius.

subsenses.def: (by extension) a person celebrated as both elite and exceptionally clever.

example.text: That guy isn't just smart, he's a peanius.

senses.def: of the highest order both in quality and in cleverness; peak and genius in equal measure.

examples.text: That plan is absolutely peanius.

notes: The on-screen text box (frame f01) is authoritative and clearly reads "Peak + Genius = Peanius," with "PEANIUS" shown as the highlighted headword; this is the lead/headline wombo of a nine-wombo rapid-fire video (others: Ouinergy, Kirking Out, Goonies, Gleak, Lowkeydokey, Bluball, Emgeekea, Sigmified). The SRT confirms the spoken roots ("Peak / Genius / Peanius"). Sendy supplies only the equation and the spoken word, not a prose definition, so the gloss ("peak quality + genius brilliance") is reconstructed directly from the two roots. ENRICHMENT (original, house-style, beyond Sendy's literal content): the adjective part of speech, the noun plural "peaniuses," subsenses 1a and 1b, the IPA, the synonyms list, and all example sentences except the literal root-equation example were composed to fit the meaning and do not contradict him. Only one entry was produced per instructions; the remaining eight wombos in the compilation were not entered.

etymology: Coined by Professor Sendy as a deliberate modern replacement for meme, which he dismisses as a millennial-era term. A blend of peak (internet-slang adjective denoting the height of quality, excellence, or comedic impact) and grins (the smiles or amused expressions such content provokes), rendered as peak + grins → Peans. The headword is normally treated as plural in form; the singular pean is implied by analogy.

senses.def: a humorous image, video, caption, or other unit of shareable internet content; the successor term to meme, proposed as a more current alternative to a word regarded as belonging to an earlier (millennial) generation.

examples.text: I love Peans.
Peans are funny.

subsenses.def: such content valued specifically for its capacity to amuse or lift the spirits; a piece of comedy consumed for reassurance or mood.

example.text: In order to brighten my day, I watch Peans.

notes: On-screen headword spelling is authoritative: "Peans," with etymology boxes reading "Peak + Grins = Peans" and the caption "Memes<Peans" (memes are inferior to / superseded by Peans). His three usage examples ("I love Peans," "Peans are funny," "In order to brighten my day, I watch Peans") are reproduced as given, lightly cleaned into sentences. House-style enrichment beyond what he literally said: the plural usage label, the implied singular form "Pean," the IPA, and the parsing of "Peak" as the slang intensifier and "Grins" as the resulting amusement (Sendy gives only the root words, not their glosses). Subsense (a) is drawn directly from his "brighten my day" example. The post also promotes an unrelated merch shirt ("Lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura") and Etsy/contact links, which are ignored as promotional boilerplate.

etymology: Blend (wombo) of PEAK + CHILL + BESTIE + HOPPERS, the four roots displayed on screen. The compound telescopes "peak chill bestie hoppers" — an admiring description of a maximally easygoing, universally befriended person — into the contracted form Pell Beppers, where pe(ak) + (chi)ll yield "pell" and b(estie) + (ho)ppers yield "beppers." The variant Pell Booper substitutes BOO for BESTIE, shifting the sense from platonic to romantic.

parts.label: slang
parts.forms: plural Pell Beppers; also used as singular Pell Bepper

senses.def: A person who is universally well liked and friendly with everyone; an effortlessly agreeable, widely beloved social figure who maintains amicable ties across an entire community.

examples.text: You're friends with Lorraine too? She's the quichest — what a Pell Bepper.
Not only is she a glazed bronut to sixty-seven different people, but she's also a straight-up Paurmisan in the brarpetorium.

subsenses.def: Used predicatively to commend the breadth and warmth of a person's friendships, often with mild astonishment at how universally accepted they are.

example.text: She's friends with absolutely everybody — a total Pell Bepper.

parts.label: slang, variant
parts.forms: Pell Booper (BESTIE → BOO); plural Pell Boopers

senses.def: A person who dates widely and frequently; one romantically involved with, or pursuing, many different people. Formed by swapping the BESTIE root of Pell Beppers for BOO, converting the sense from platonic popularity to romantic promiscuity.

examples.text: He's a straight-up Pell Booper.
He went on a date in the park, it was quiche-loreraining cats and dogs, he had a ponchalashout all over the parkench, and then he broke up with her.

notes: On-screen headword and etymology are clearly legible: 'Pell Beppers' over the blue roots 'Peak + Chill + Bestie + Hoppers'. House-style enrichment beyond his literal words: the dry plural/singular form notes, the usage labels ('slang', 'variant'), the parenthetical phonological gloss in the etymology (how the roots contract into 'pell'/'beppers'), the subsense 1a, and the synonym list (only 'glazed bronut', which he uses in-context, and the generic 'social butterfly'). IPA is a plausible reconstruction in the dry register. Note: many of Sendy's example sentences are themselves stacked with other wombos (quichest, glazed bronut, Paurmisan, brarpetorium, quiche-loreraining, ponchalashout, parkench, brousin, Wombomboclat) which appear here only as verbatim context for the Pell Beppers/Pell Booper usage, not as the headword. The Etsy merch tie-in references a different word ('Lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura'), unrelated to this entry.

etymology: A blend (wombo) of perchance ("by chance; perhaps; possibly") and happenstance ("a circumstance regarded as coincidental or due to chance"). Perchance descends from Middle English per chance, from Anglo-French par chance ("by chance"); happenstance is a 19th-century American coinage formed from happen + the ending of circumstance. Coined by Professor Sendy, who fuses the two near-synonymous notions of chance into a single word so that the speaker may, on the off chance and as luck would have it, propose doing something.

senses.def: to do something on the off chance or as a happy coincidence; to perhaps, by some turn of fortune, engage in an activity, especially as floated to another as a casual invitation.

examples.text: I asked my rendezboo if she would perchappenstance at the dancisco.
Do you perchappenstance wanna go perchappenstancemaxxing this tweekend?

subsenses.def: (transitive, with infinitive) to propose tentatively that someone might, by chance, join in an undertaking.

example.text: I asked her if she would perchappenstance to do some twirkmaxxing.

senses.def: perhaps; maybe; by some chance or fortunate coincidence — used to soften or hedge a proposal.

examples.text: Do you perchappenstance wanna go to the dancisco?

notes: On-screen black box reads "Perchappenstance"; blue etymology box reads "Perchance + Happenstance" (verified in frame f02). Upload date 20260430, so year 2026. Sendy demonstrates the word primarily as a verb ("would she perchappenstance," "go perchappenstancemaxxing") and also adverbially as a hedge ("do you perchappenstance wanna go"); both parts of speech are drawn from his actual usage. The examples are his real caption lines, lightly cleaned into sentences and stripped of the nested wombos (rendezboo, dancisco, twirkmaxxing, tweekend, perchappenstancemaxxing) where they would obscure the headword's meaning, except where retained to show authentic context. House-style enrichment beyond what he literally said: the inflected verb forms, the IPA, the adverb sense label, and subsense 1a (transitive-with-infinitive shade) are editorial additions consistent with his demonstrated meaning. Both senses converge on the idea of "perhaps, by chance" since perchance and happenstance are near-synonyms of fortuity.

etymology: A triple-stacked wombo built by Professor Sendy from three intermediate compounds that themselves share the common emphatic tail "-genuinely": Perchenuinely + Kirkenuinely + Burgenuinely, telescoped into a single uninterrupted lexeme. In the second, extended form the word absorbs a further root, Vibonoculearnableundereppyeakohotictrance, to yield Perkirkenburgenuibonoculearnableundereppyeakohotictrance — an exercise in recursive root-accretion in which each segment is fused at its overlapping seam rather than hyphenated.

subsenses.def: as a stacked superlative formed by fusing several lesser emphatics (perchenuinely, kirkenuinely, burgenuinely) into one continuous adverb.
in its further-extended form, denoting an emphasis carried to the point of absurd, ever-accreting excess.

etymology: A blend (wombo) of POTTY (as in "potty mouth," childishly crude or scatological speech; ultimately from pot, a chamber vessel) and LANGUAGE (from Old French langage, from Latin lingua "tongue"). Coined by Professor Sendy as a self-deprecating label for the vulgar, shock-driven register he wishes to keep the wombo form from collapsing into.

senses.def: Crude, vulgar, or scatological language, especially humor that leans on shock value or off-color subject matter; potty-mouthed speech.

examples.text: I don't want wombos to turn into a potty language.
Some of the ones people send me are indeed really funny, but they're too far for me, too deep into pottanguage.

subsenses.def: (of a coinage or comic form) the degraded state a clever invention falls into when it abandons craft for cheap crudeness, becoming meaningless and quick to grow stale.

example.text: I don't want wombos to turn into pottanguage that's meaningless and just becomes a hilarious joke that gets old really quick.

subsenses.def: the risque register that a careful coiner deliberately rides the edge of without crossing; the line between tasteful innuendo and outright vulgarity.

example.text: I ride the line sometimes with that, but I'm not gonna take the pottanguage too, too far.

senses.def: to debase an invented word or comic form by steering it toward crude, vulgar, or shock-driven content.

examples.text: A wombo loses its art the moment you pottanguage it.

The verb sense (to pottanguage), the agent noun (pottanguager), and IPA are house-style enrichment consistent with his usage; the second sense (b) generalizing to the decay of a coinage trend is mild original extension flagged here. Confidence medium because the headword is unambiguous from the frame but the sense is inferred from a meta-commentary rather than a standard definitional video.

A wombo in the canonical Sendy mold: two roots smashed at a sound they hold in common.

The blend overwrites the medical homograph "prostate," repurposing the existing word to denote a heightened condition of expert focus. Presented in Sendy's "Must Know Community Wombos" compilation (2026), a roundup of viewer-submitted formations.

notes: SOURCE IS A COMPILATION, NOT A SINGLE-WORD DEFINITION. This video ("MUST KNOW COMMUNITY WOMBOS", uploaded 2026-06-08) is a roundup of eight community-submitted wombos, each shown only as an etymology formula with no spoken definition or usage sentence. The on-screen formulas are: (1) Professional + Flowstate = Prostate; (2) Sigma + Spectacular = Sigtacular; (3) Peak + Cinema + Analysis + Multiverse = Peaneverse; (4) We + AF + Lowkey + AF = Weafloaf; (5) Free + Key + Peak + Cinema + Analysis = Freekey Peanemis; (6) Ball + Real + OG = Balrog; (7) Subpeona + Peak + Cinema + Analysis = Subpeonemis; (8) Me + AF + Flowstate + AF = Meaflowf State. I selected "Prostate" as the lead/headline entry (first listed, first read in captions). The DEFINITION below is INFERRED from the source roots (Professional + Flowstate) per house style, because Sendy never states a meaning or gives a usage example for it; this is enrichment, hence low confidence. "Balrog" notably collides with the Tolkien/Street Fighter proper noun; "Subpeonemis" preserves the community's misspelling of "subpoena" (shown on-screen as "Subpeona"). The headword spelling "Prostate" is taken authoritatively from frame f02.

etymology: A blend (wombo) of QUICHEST (the superlative of QUICHE, itself a wombo of QUIRKY + NICHE) + OF + ALL + TIME, formed in deliberate imitation of the established acronym GOAT ("Greatest Of All Time"). Coined by Professor Sendy in 2026 as part of a montage of "brand new wombos." The substitution of the initial Q- for the G- of GOAT signals that the honoree's distinction lies not in greatness but in being the most charmingly quirky-and-niche of any contender. Compare the closely related wombo QOATED (Quiche + Goated), in which the same Q-for-G swap is applied to the participial GOATED.

senses.def: the single most quirky-and-niche exemplar within a given field or category; the one regarded, of all time, as supreme in idiosyncratic and specialist appeal. A reverent play on GOAT in which excellence is measured by quirkiness rather than greatness.

examples.text: Quichest of all time — the QOAT.

subsenses.def: (with reference to a person) one celebrated as the foremost connoisseur or practitioner of an obscure or offbeat pursuit.

example.text: His record collection is all forgotten B-sides; the man is simply the QOAT.

subsenses.def: (with reference to a thing) an object, work, or phenomenon held to be the most endearingly niche of its kind.

example.text: A lecture series on medieval cheese law: genuinely the QOAT.

senses.def: of or relating to the highest order of quirky-and-niche distinction; that is the QOAT.

examples.text: That's a QOAT take if I've ever heard one.

I selected QOAT as the headword because it leads both the title and the on-screen list. On-screen formula (authoritative): "Quichest + Of + All + Time = QOAT", and the related "Quiche + Goated = Qoated". The auto-captions render the on-screen text faithfully here, so frame and SRT agree. Sendy's only literal content is the formula and the spoken tag "all time the QOAT"; he gives no spoken definition, so the meaning is inferred directly from the formula (Quichest Of All Time, modeled on GOAT). ENRICHMENT I added in house style: sub-senses 1a/1b (person vs. thing), all illustrative example sentences (Sendy provides no usage examples for this word, so these are original but consistent with the meaning), the attributive-adjective part of speech and its sense, the noun forms/plural, and the IPA. The cross-reference to QOATED (a sibling wombo from the same video) and to the parent wombo QUICHE (Quirky + Niche) is drawn directly from the on-screen frame.

etymology: Blend of QUIRKY and NICHE, the two source words colliding so that the medial syllables fuse and the spelling lands on the pre-existing word "quiche" (a savoury egg tart, from French quiche, ultimately from Alsatian German Küchen "cake"). Coined by Professor Sendy; the homograph with the dish is incidental and is exploited for deadpan effect. First attested 2026.

parts.label: slang
parts.forms: comparative more quiche; superlative most quiche

senses.def: Possessing an appeal that is at once quirky and niche; offbeat and idiosyncratic in a way that finds a small but devoted audience rather than a mainstream one.

examples.text: Professor Sendy is quiche.

subsenses.def: (of a person or creator) endearingly eccentric and specialised; the sort whose work rewards the cognoscenti.

example.text: You should go watch his videos and get back to me with the full analysis.

subsenses.def: (of content, taste, or aesthetics) charmingly off the beaten path; unusual in a curated, deliberate fashion.

example.text: His whole feed is quiche.

senses.def: A person or thing exhibiting quiche quality; one whose charm lies in being simultaneously quirky and niche. House enrichment of the adjectival sense.

examples.text: He's a total quiche, and that's exactly why people watch.

notes: This is a four-word compilation post; the on-screen frame shows four wombos (Quirky + Niche = Quiche; Bro + Professor = Brofessor; Sus + Success = Susess; Sigma + Tuff = Sigmuff). Per the one-entry instruction, the headword is QUICHE, which leads the caption, the frame, and the audio. The definition reflects what Sendy actually says: he applies it to himself ("Professor Sendy is Quiche"), i.e. simultaneously quirky and niche. Enrichment flagged: (1) the second pronunciation /ˈkwɪʃ/ is a plausible house-style spelling-pronunciation retaining the QUIRKY onset, since the dish-homograph /kiːʃ/ is the obvious reading; (2) the derived adverb "quichely" is original house enrichment consistent with the adjective and is not spoken by Sendy. The captions also contain three other coinages not defined here (peanemis = peak + cinema + analysis; cringema = cringe + cinema; and an undefined "Nonchalashout"), plus the merch word "Lowkirkentologiclowstatothenticlaura." Sendy's literal sense is preserved; dictionary prose is original.

etymology: A compound wombo coined by Professor Sendy. The first element, quiche, is itself a blend of quirky + niche, denoting something offbeat and specialized; the second element, loreain, blends lore + explain, denoting the recounting of backstory. The full form puns on quiche Lorraine, the savory egg tart, with the homophonous loreain supplying a verbal sense. In its later use the term migrates from the act of explaining to the act of forcefully hurling, the egg-tart imagery lending itself to the notion of something being flung.

parts.label: slang
parts.forms: quiche loreains; quiche loreained; quiche loreaining (also clipped loreain)

senses.def: to set out the backstory or origins of something or someone for the benefit of another person; to narrate the lore behind a subject.

examples.text: When a newcomer asks who that is, you quiche loreain the whole saga for them.

subsenses.def: to explain, in informal use, with an emphasis on context and history rather than mere facts.

example.text: Let me loreain it real quick so the backstory makes sense.

senses.def: to hurl or fling an object at a person deliberately and with considerable force; to throw something pointedly, often unexpectedly.

examples.text: He just quiche loreained the remote across the room at me.
It's kind of like yeeting it, but more intentional, with more force behind it.

subsenses.def: distinguished from yeet by the deliberateness of the act and the greater power of the throw.

example.text: You don't just toss it, you loreain it.

senses.def: an act of explaining the backstory of a thing or person to another.

examples.text: She gave the new hire a full quiche loreain on how the team got here.

senses.def: a forceful, intentional throw of an object at someone.

examples.text: That was a clean quiche loreain straight at his head.

notes: On-screen title reads "Quiche Loreain / 2nd Meaning," confirming the headword spelling and that this video extends an existing wombo rather than coining a fresh one. The SRT preserves the etymology cards (Quiche = Quirky + Niche; loreain = Lore + Explain) at the head of the clip. Sendy explicitly states the original sense (explaining backstory) before introducing the second sense (forceful, intentional throwing), so both senses are given here. The verb part of speech and the inflected forms (loreained, loreaining) are tasteful house-style enrichment consistent with his usage "you loreain it" and "quiche loreain it"; Sendy demonstrates the base verb form but does not pronounce the inflections. The comparison to "yeet" is his own. IPA for loreain follows the homophony with the place-name Lorraine that the joke depends on.

etymology: A compound wombo blending Quiche (itself a portmanteau of QUIRKY + NICHE) with the chained elements LORE + ANALYSIS + REVISIONS, fused as Loreanalvisions. The whole denotes "quirky niche lore-analysis revisions." Coined by Professor Sendy (2026). Sendy explicitly notes that the Lore element is swappable: substituting FUTURE yields the companion form Quiche Futanalvisions (Quirky + Niche + Future + Analysis + Revisions), applied to revised predictions rather than revised history.

senses.def: A quirky, niche revision of past events or established backstory; an idiosyncratic after-the-fact reanalysis by which one rewrites the record of what previously happened.

examples.text: He was dropping Quiche Loreanalvisions about how he had all these rendezboos in like sixth or seventh grade.
I was like, bro, back then you never even entered the Huzzitorium; you were a little too Quiche for the ladies.

subsenses.def: Used specifically of a self-flattering reinvention of one's own personal history.

example.text: His Quiche Loreanalvisions painted his awkward middle-school years as a string of conquests.

senses.def: In the variant form Quiche Futanalvisions (substituting future for lore): a quirky, niche revision of a previously stated prediction or forecast.

examples.text: These would be Quiche Futanalvisions; for example, he had a LeBronchalashout over the clavarpet when he made that Quiche Futanalvision about how in forty-one years it'll be the sixorseveno'clockalypse.

senses.def: To perform a Quiche Loreanalvision; to revise or reanalyze one's own past in a quirky, niche manner. (See notes.)

examples.text: First you need to do a futanal, then a lorision, and then a slickly placed Kirkumcision.

notes: On-screen black box reads "Quiche Loreanalvisions"; blue etymology boxes read "Quirky + Niche + Lore + Analysis + Revisions" (the shirt graphic also spells LOREANALVISIONS), so the spelling is authoritative over the auto-caption "Loreanalvisions." Core meaning ("use this wombo when somebody revises the past") is taken directly from the captions, rewritten in house style. Sense 2 (Quiche Futanalvisions) is Sendy's own explicit variant for revised predictions. The verb part of speech is tasteful house-style enrichment: Sendy uses the word only as a noun, but he models related verb-like coinages (futanal, lorision) in the same breath; the closing example is his actual line. Sense 1a (self-flattering reinvention) is a mild house-style shading drawn from his "rendezboos in sixth or seventh grade" usage. The video chains several other one-off wombos (rendezboos, brousin, huzzitorium, LeBronchalashout, clavarpet, sixorseveno'clockalypse, Kirkumcision) which are incidental and not the headword. Upload date 20260426 sets the year to 2026.

etymology: Blend coined by Professor Sendy. The base term peanemis is a compression of peak cinema analysis (peak + cinema + analysis → pea·nem·is). Quiche, here a homophonic respelling of quirky-niche (quirky + niche), is prefixed to specify a peanemis of an idiosyncratic, off-mainstream character. The derived verb quichemaxx joins quiche with the intensive suffix -maxx (from the "looksmaxxing" family, denoting deliberate optimization to the maximum degree).

parts.label: the base form
parts.forms: peanemis; with quirky-niche slant quiche peanemis

senses.def: A critical rundown or considered opinion of something notable that a person has experienced, especially a film, series, or other media; an informal piece of peak cinema analysis.

subsenses.def: Specifically, an account of one's verdict on a piece of cinema or video content.

example.text: I haven't seen Palm Beach Pete's videos. Could you please just give me the peanemis?

subsenses.def: By extension, a debrief on any cool event or thing one has been present for, not limited to film.

example.text: Bro, go to the party and please deliver the peanemis.

examples.text: Use this wombo when you want to know somebody's opinion about something cool that they experienced.

senses.def: (in the form quiche peanemis) A peanemis delivered with a quirky, niche, or off-mainstream sensibility; a rundown that is deliberately less straightforward and more idiosyncratic.

subsenses.def: chiefly imperative, in 'deliver the quiche peanemis': a request for such a take.

example.text: Please deliver the quiche peanemis.

examples.text: If you want that peanemis to be a little bit more quirky and niche, throw quiche in before the peanemis.

senses.def: transitive: to render a peanemis maximally quirky and niche; to dial up the off-mainstream, idiosyncratic quality of an analysis to its fullest extent.

examples.text: We could even take it a step further by quichemaxxing it out.
Could you please quichemaxx this peanemis?

notes: On-screen headword is authoritatively "Quiche Peanemis" with etymology boxes "(Quirky + Niche)" and "(Peak + Cinema + Analysis)"; the auto-caption rendered "Peanemis" consistently, matching the frame, so no spelling conflict. Year 2026 from upload_date 20260326. Senses, parts of speech, and examples are drawn directly from Sendy: peanemis = peak cinema analysis / someone's opinion on a cool thing they experienced; the imperative "deliver/give the peanemis"; quiche = quirky-niche modifier; and the verb quichemaxx ("could you please quichemaxx this peanemis"). ENRICHMENT I added in house style: the IPA reconstructions; the usage labels ("often imperative," "informal," "chiefly in 'give/deliver the ___'"); the explicit gloss of -maxx in the etymology; the noun-form forms fields; subsense 1b (extending peanemis from strictly cinematic to any "cool thing experienced," which Sendy implies with his party example); the synonym list; and the cross-reference framing of quichemaxx as the intensive verb. None of this contradicts Sendy's stated meaning. The "Palm Beach Pete" and "bro went to a party" examples are his actual caption usages, lightly cleaned.

etymology: A compound coinage blending five elements: QUIRKY + NICHE + CURIOUS + PEAK + SHIZZ. The first portion, "Quichous," fuses the opening sounds of "quirky," "niche," and "curious" into a single mock-French-looking head element (suggestive of "quiche"), while the second portion, "Peashizz," joins "peak" (slang for excellent, at its height) with "shizz" (a softened, emphatic form of slang affirmation). Coined by Professor Sendy.

senses.def: Something perceived as remarkably cool, novel, and impressive; an object, event, or person that is at once offbeat, specialized, intriguing, and excellent.

examples.text: He deadassionately dropped the Quichous Peashizz, and I was like, brollahi.

subsenses.def: Used as an exclamation or appraisal on encountering such a thing.

example.text: Just when you couldn't get any more quiche, this twella shows up.

senses.def: An expression of admiration or delight uttered upon witnessing something exceptionally cool and new.

examples.text: Quichous Peashizz!

notes: On-screen headword and five-root etymology (Quirky + Niche + Curious + Peak + Shizz) taken authoritatively from frame f02. Definition paraphrased from Sendy's spoken caption: "Use this wombo when you see something really cool and new." The interjection part of speech is light house-style enrichment, supported by his exclamatory usage example ("Just when you couldn't get any more quiche, this twella shows up"). IPA is a plausible dry-register reconstruction; the first variant reflects the "quiche"-like reading he plays on, the second a more literal "quirky" onset. The humorous mispronunciation "Queeshees Peasheez" is his own (caption lines 31). The video is dense with other wombos used illustratively (deadassionately, brollahi, twella, twother, clavother, capsolutely, bizarrpet, flopeanemis) but those are separate coinages, not this headword.

etymology: Coined by Professor Sendy in 2026, reportedly from a viewer's top comment in the "Top Comment Becomes a WOMBO" segment. A blend (wombo) of READY + (bur)RITO / -ITO, the burrito ending grafted onto "ready" so that the word announces its own convenience the way "ready, set, go" announces a start.

senses.def: a burrito engineered for instant consumption; a pre-made, often microwaveable burrito requiring no preparation beyond heating.

examples.text: When the countdown hit zero he was already eating his Readeto.

subsenses.def: by extension, any meal or foodstuff valued chiefly for being ready the instant one is.

example.text: He keeps a drawer of Readetos for nights he cannot cook.

senses.def: uttered at the conclusion of a 'ready, set, go' countdown to signal that the thing in question is, at that very moment, prepared and available.

examples.text: Ready, set, go - Readeto!

notes: SEVERE SOURCING LIMITATION — flag for review.
